The Reader (2008)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $34,194,407Details
International Box Office $78,770,468
Worldwide Box Office $112,964,875
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $13,226,311
Total $13,226,311
Further financial details...

Synopsis

When he falls ill on his way home from school, 15 year-old Michael Berg is rescued by Hanna, a woman twice his age. The two begin an unexpected and passionate affair only for Hanna to suddenly and inexplicably disappear. Eight years later, Michael, now a young law student observing Nazi war trials, meets his former lover again, under very different circumstances. Hanna is on trial for a hideous crime, and as she refuses to defend herself, Michael gradually realizes his boyhood love may be guarding a secret she considers to be more shameful than murder.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$168,051 (0.5% of total gross)
Legs:11.95 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:30.3% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$32,500,000 (worldwide box office is 3.5 times production budget)
Theater counts:8 opening theaters/1,203 max. theaters, 8.8 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $54,667,629

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: December 10th, 2008 (Limited) by Weinstein Co.
International Releases: March 26th, 2009 (Limited) (South Korea)
Video Release: April 14th, 2009 by Weinstein Co./Genius
MPA Rating: R for some scenes of sexuality and nudity
Running Time: 122 minutes
Award nomination: 2009 Oscars Best Picture Nominee
Plot point: February / May Romance, Illiteracy / Learning to Read, Suicide
Social setting: Holocaust
Subgenre: Romantic Drama
Time period setting: 1950s, 1960s, 1980s
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Mirage Enterprises, Neunte Babelsberg Films GmbH
Production Countries: Germany, United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

When he falls ill on his way home from school, 15 year-old Michael Berg is rescued by Hanna, a woman twice his age. The two begin an unexpected and passionate affair only for Hanna to suddenly and inexplicably disappear. Eight years later, Michael, now a young law student observing Nazi war trials, meets his former lover again, under very different circumstances. Hanna is on trial for a hideous crime, and as she refuses to defend herself, Michael gradually realizes his boyhood love may be guarding a secret she considers to be more shameful than murder.
